title: USA vs. Paraguay lineups: Projected USMNT starting 11, team news for World Cup 2026 opener | NESN
original_text:
Kyle Bonn is a Syracuse University broadcast journalism graduate with over a decade of experience covering soccer globally. Kyle specializes in soccer tactics and betting, with a degree in data analytics. Kyle also does TV broadcasts for Wake Forest soccer, and has had previous stops with NBC Soccer and IMG College. When not covering the game, he has long enjoyed loyalty to the New York Giants, Yankees, and Fulham. Kyle enjoys playing racquetball and video games when not watching or covering sports.

Carlan Gay is a NESN writer covering sports news, analysis and live events. He also serves as an assistant managing editor at The Sporting News, covering the NFL, NBA, college athletics and global sports. In addition, Gay is a freelance play-by-play announcer who has called international basketball for FIBA Media since 2017, along with NCAA basketball, NCAA wrestling, international hockey, baseball and more. You can follow him on Twitter at @TheCarlanGay and on Bluesky at @carlangay.

The United States begins its 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against Paraguay in a Group D matchup at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles.

With the tournament being played on home soil, expectations will be high for Mauricio Pochettino and the USMNT. The Americans have reached the quarterfinals just once in the modern World Cup era, doing so in 2002, and will be hoping to make their deepest run yet in 2026.

Christian Pulisic is expected to lead a U.S. squad looking to make the most of home-field advantage and build momentum with a strong performance in its opening match.

Here's a breakdown of the projected USA vs. Paraguay lineups, team news and World Cup squads and information on how to watch the Group D match.

LIVE: Follow USMNT vs. Paraguay updates from 2026 World Cup

USA projected starting lineup (4-2-3-1, right to left): GK : 24. Matt Freese DEF : 16. Alex Freeman, 3. Chris Richards, 13. Tim Ream, 5. Antonee Robinson MID : 8. Weston McKennie, 4. Tyler Adams AM : 2. Sergino Dest, 17. Malik Tillman, 10. Christian Pulisic FWD : 20. Folarin Balogun

Chris Richards appears ready to return to the heart of the USA defense after missing the USMNT’s final two World Cup warm-up matches against Senegal and Germany with an ankle injury. The Crystal Palace center-back returned to full team training ahead of the Paraguay match and is expected to start alongside captain Tim Ream.

Mauricio Pochettino could use a fluid 4-2-3-1 formation, with Sergino Dest beginning in an advanced position on the right. Alex Freeman’s versatility allows Dest to push higher up the field while the back line shifts into a three-player structure during periods of possession.

Tyler Adams and Weston McKennie are projected to anchor the midfield, with Malik Tillman operating behind Folarin Balogun. Christian Pulisic should start from the left but will have the freedom to move inside and become the USMNT’s primary attacking threat.

Cameron Carter-Vickers and Patrick Agyemang were unavailable for World Cup selection after suffering Achilles injuries, while an ankle injury kept Johnny Cardoso out of the final 26-player USA squad.

Paraguay projected starting lineup (4-2-3-1): GK : 1. Roberto Fernandez DEF : 4. Juan Caceres, 15. Gustavo Gomez, 3. Omar Alderete, 6. Junior Alonso MID : 16. Damian Bobadilla, 14. Andres Cubas AM : 8. Diego Gomez, 19. Julio Enciso, 10. Miguel Almiron FWD : 9. Antonio Sanabria

Julio Enciso is the biggest question surrounding Paraguay’s starting lineup. The Strasbourg attacker was stretchered off during the first half of a 4-0 warm-up victory over Nicaragua, initially raising concerns that he could miss a significant portion of the World Cup.

Enciso has since made encouraging progress, however, and coach Gustavo Alfaro has left the door open for him to start against the United States. His availability is likely to be determined close to kickoff. If Enciso is unable to begin the match, Paraguay could move Diego Gomez into a more central attacking role and add another wide player.

Gomez will carry plenty of Paraguay’s creative responsibility either way. The Brighton midfielder previously played alongside Lionel Messi at Inter Miami and can carry the ball forward, break into the penalty area, and support Antonio Sanabria.

At the other end of the field, captain Gustavo Gomez anchors a defense that was the foundation of Paraguay’s successful qualifying campaign. The 33-year-old Palmeiras center-back is approaching 90 international appearances and should partner Omar Alderete in the middle of the back four.

Alfaro has moved between a 4-2-3-1 and 4-4-2, but Paraguay’s approach generally remains the same. They defend in a compact shape, protect the middle of the field and look to release Miguel Almiron and their other attackers quickly on the counter.

Below are the complete World Cup 2026 rosters for the United States and Paraguay, including each player’s position, club and international experience. Cap totals reflect the official squad announcement dates and may change as the tournament progresses.

Soccer fans in the United States can watch USA vs. Paraguay live on FOX. The World Cup match will also be available to stream through FOX One and other live-TV streaming services that carry FOX.

One option is Fubo, which includes FOX in participating markets and offers a free trial to eligible new subscribers. Fans can sign up, stream the USMNT’s World Cup opener live and cancel at any time.
